pattern: Optimal play reduces to a parity check on the number of blanks plus a fixed balance equation between the two halves.
hints: Track, for each half, the sum of known digits and how many blanks it has.
If the total number of blanks is odd, the first player can always force inequality.
Otherwise the second player can force equality only when nine times the blank difference equals twice the known-sum difference between the halves.

edge_inputs.description: anchor example no blanks already equal
anchor example two blanks Alice wins
anchor example balanced blanks Bob wins
two blanks split across halves can balance
single blank odd count first player wins

stress.description: random even-length strings of digits and blanks
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    half = max(1, size // 2)
    n = 2 * half
    chars = '0123456789?'
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(chars) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
